stories.title: CPSC Recall — KTEBO Writing Tablets (Battery Ingestion) + Zippee Silicone Activity Toys (Choking)

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
RECALL: KTEBO LCD writing tablets recalled — battery screw can loosen, exposing a button cell battery. Swallowing one can cause burns or death.

Stop use now. Free replacement: writing-tablet-recall@ktebo.com

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Also: Zippee silicone fidget toys (Mobi Games cylinder with ball-ended strings) recalled for choking risk. 117,000+ units sold on Amazon 2019–2024.

Full refund: cpsc.gov/Recalls

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

article.headline: Two Toy Recalls This Week: KTEBO Writing Tablets and Zippee Silicone Toys

research.facts:
CPSC Recalls — KTEBO Writing Tablets + Zippee Silicone Toys
**Sources:**
- CPSC.gov (official): KTEBO Writing Tablet recall page
- CPSC.gov (official): Zippee Silicone Activity Toys recall page
- NewsNation: KTEBO recall coverage
- ABC27: KTEBO recall coverage
- Motherly: Zippee recall coverage
- GrowingYourBaby.com: Zippee recall coverage
**Verified facts:**
- KTEBO Writing Tablet Toys: battery compartment screw does not stay attached; button/coin battery ingestion risk (chemical burns and death) — **HIGH** (CPSC.gov + NewsNation + ABC27)
- ~10,380 units sold on Amazon (Sept-Oct 2025, $8-$30) — **HIGH** (CPSC.gov)
- 8.5" and 10" sizes in multiple color combinations — **HIGH** (CPSC.gov)
- Action: Stop use; contact writing-tablet-recall@ktebo.com for free replacement set — **HIGH** (CPSC.gov)
- Zippee Silicone Activity Toys by Mobi Games: ball-ended silicone strings can reach back of child's throat — **HIGH** (CPSC.gov + Motherly + GrowingYourBaby)
- ~117,000-143,000 units sold at Amazon, PlayMobi.com, specialty toy stores (Nov 2019-Jan 2024, ~$20) — **HIGH** (CPSC.gov)
- One confirmed incident of a child gagging and vomiting — **HIGH** (CPSC.gov)
- Date codes inside cylinder in MMDDYY format 030620–110823 — **HIGH** (CPSC.gov)
- Action: Stop use; contact Mobi Games for full refund — **HIGH** (CPSC.gov)
- Both violate mandatory toy safety standards — **HIGH** (CPSC.gov)
**Do NOT use:** Exact units sold (range varies by source for Zippee — use "more than 117,000").

stories.title: LFTE USA Playground Swing Seats Recall + YITA Dresser Tip-Over Recall

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
COS parents: playground swing seats at parks/schools are recalled (LFTE USA, model 999604). Rivet can fail — fall hazard.

If your school or local park has these, report it to your district or parks dept.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Recall: YITA + 5 fabric-frame dresser brands — tip-over hazard, STURDY Act violation.

~6,800 units on Amazon + Walmart 2023–2025. Stop use if not wall-anchored. Full refund available.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

article.headline: Playground Swing Seats and Kids' Dressers Recalled: What COS Families Should Know

research.facts:
LFTE USA Playground Swings + YITA Dresser Recalls
**Sources:**
- CPSC.gov (official): LFTE USA Playground Swing Set Seats recall page
- CPSC.gov (official): YITA Multi-Brand Dresser recall page
- Yahoo/ABC27: LFTE USA coverage
- MyParisTexas: LFTE USA coverage
- furniture-tip-over-accidents.com: YITA coverage
- Newsweek: YITA coverage
**Verified facts:**
- LFTE USA Playground Swing Set Seats: rivets supporting swing seat can fail; one confirmed child injury — **HIGH** (CPSC.gov + Yahoo/ABC27 + MyParisTexas)
- ~7,200 units; sold to Miracle Recreation Equipment and Little Tikes Commercial (Jan-Sept 2025, $80-$95) — **HIGH** (CPSC.gov)
- Model number 999604 ONLY; colors: black, blue, green, red, tan, yellow — **HIGH** (CPSC.gov)
- Sold to commercial/institutional buyers, not individual consumers — affects public playgrounds and school equipment — **HIGH** (CPSC.gov)
- Action: Stop use; contact recall@lfteusa.com for free replacement — **HIGH** (CPSC.gov)
- YITA/Dextrus/ModFusion/Uforic/Yintatech/Yitahome 16-drawer dressers: tip-over and entrapment hazard; violate STURDY Act mandatory standard — **HIGH** (CPSC.gov + furniture-tip-over + Newsweek)
- ~6,800 units; sold Amazon and Walmart (Sept 2023-Aug 2025, $75-$100); colors: gray, black, white, pink, brown — **HIGH** (CPSC.gov)
- Action: Stop using if not wall-anchored; keep away from children; contact YITA for full refund — **HIGH** (CPSC.gov)
**Do NOT use:** Claims that specific COS parks or schools have these swings (not confirmed).

stories.title: D49 Budget Crisis — Fiscal Exigency, 39 Jobs Cut, $2M Admin Error

x_posts.label: Post 3-1
x_posts.text:
D49 families: the district cut 39 positions (12 tenured teachers) after declaring fiscal exigency. Then a $2M admin accounting error was found — no one lost their job over it.

Here's the full timeline: 👇

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 3-2
x_posts.text:
D49 timeline: fiscal exigency (Jan), $1.8M cuts + 12 tenured teachers nonrenewed (Feb), state audit finds $2M enrollment reporting error (March).

39 jobs gone. No admin accountability.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

article.headline: D49 Budget Crisis: What Falcon School District Families Need to Know

research.facts:
D49 Budget Crisis
**Sources:**
- KOAA: "We are stripping the district down to absolute essentials" article
- KRDO: $2M administrative error investigation piece
- Rocky Mountain PBS: D49 teacher cuts coverage
- Pikes Peak Bulletin: Fiscal exigency coverage
- Colorado Springs Gazette: Jan. 17 board decision coverage
**Verified facts:**
- Jan 8, 2026: D49 board voted 4-1 to declare fiscal exigency — **HIGH** (Gazette + Pikes Peak Bulletin)
- Reasons cited: flat revenue, declining enrollment, rising healthcare/operating costs, defunding of mill levy equalization — **HIGH** (KOAA + Gazette)
- Feb 2026: Board approved $1.8M in cuts — 39 positions eliminated, 12 tenured teacher contracts nonrenewed — **HIGH** (KOAA + RMPBS + Pikes Peak Bulletin)
- March 2026: KRDO investigation revealed 2025 state audit found D49 incorrectly reported student FTE counts due to bell schedule errors — **HIGH** (KRDO)
- State recovering ~$2.66M overpayment — **HIGH** (KRDO)
- D49 faced ~$2M fine — **HIGH** (KRDO)
- No administrators lost their jobs over the accounting error — **HIGH** (KRDO)
**Do NOT use:**
- Specific teacher or administrator names without direct verification
- D49 Student Success Center (MEDIUM, separate story, not confirmed for this article)

stories.title: AAP vs. CDC: What the 2026 Vaccine Schedule Split Means for COS Families

x_posts.label: Post 4-1
x_posts.text:
The AAP and CDC now recommend DIFFERENT childhood vaccine schedules.

AAP: 18 diseases. CDC: 11. Key gap — COVID-19 and HPV dosing. AAP backed by 12 medical orgs including the AMA.

Talk to your pediatrician.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 4-2
x_posts.text:
Your pediatrician may now follow the AAP schedule, not the CDC's.

Key differences: COVID-19 (AAP recommends universally for ages 6–23 months) and HPV dosing. Ask which schedule they're using.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

article.headline: AAP and CDC Now Have Different Vaccine Schedules: What Colorado Springs Parents Should Know

research.facts:
AAP vs. CDC Vaccine Schedule
**Sources:**
- AAP News (official publication): 2026 immunization schedule article
- AJMC: AAP breaks with CDC coverage
- Vaccine Advisor / Clinical Advisor: AAP maintains routine recs
- CHOC Children's Health Hub: AAP 2026 schedule summary
- Contemporary OB/GYN: AAP schedule release coverage
**Verified facts:**
- AAP 2026 schedule recommends vaccinations for 18 diseases — **HIGH** (AAP + AJMC + CHOC)
- CDC 2026 revised schedule covers 11 diseases — **HIGH** (AAP statement + AJMC)
- CDC changes prompted by Trump administration directive to align with international standards (benchmarked against 20 peer nations) — **HIGH** (AJMC)
- COVID-19: AAP recommends universal vaccination all children 6-23 months + risk-based ages 2-17 — **HIGH** (AAP + CHOC)
- COVID-19: CDC places in "shared clinical decision-making" — **HIGH** (AJMC + CHOC)
- HPV: AAP recommends 2 doses starting ages 9-12 — **HIGH** (AAP + CHOC)
- HPV: CDC recommends 1 dose at ages 11-12 — **HIGH** (AJMC)
- AAP statement: CDC changes "depart from longstanding medical evidence and no longer offer the optimal way to prevent illnesses in children" — **HIGH** (AAP official statement, multiple sources)
- AAP schedule endorsed by 12 medical associations including AMA, ACOG, IDSA — **HIGH** (AAP + AJMC)
**Do NOT use:**
- Specific AAP spokesperson names without direct verification
- Claims about Colorado-specific vaccine rates (not found)
- Any strong advocacy for/against either schedule — present both sides neutrally

Day-use fee applies. 50+ miles of trails.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

article.headline: Mueller State Park Junior Rangers Programs and COS Spring Trail Updates for Families

research.facts:
Mueller State Park Junior Rangers + Spring Trail Guide
**Sources:**
- Colorado Springs Gazette (March 19, 2026): Mueller State Park spring hiking guide
- KOAA: Colorado Parks and Wildlife Mueller spring programs
- Colorado Parks and Wildlife (official): Mueller State Park page
- coloradosprings.gov/TOPS2026: 2026 city trails plan
- KRDO: TOPS 2026 trail plan coverage
- Colorado Springs Gazette (Jan. 4): TOPS 2026 plan
**Verified facts:**
- Mueller State Park "Junior Rangers in the Forest": April 18; learn wild plants and animals; earn Jr. Ranger badge; ages 6-12 — **HIGH** (Gazette + KOAA + CPW)
- "Basic Survival for Kids": April 10; safety tips; indoor/outdoor depending on weather — **HIGH** (Gazette + KOAA)
- "Junior Rangers on the Trail": April 4 (past but useful context for ongoing program) — **HIGH**
- Mueller: 50+ miles of trails; 5,117 acres — **HIGH** (CPW official)
- Mueller approximately 35 miles from downtown COS — **MEDIUM** (general geography, not confirmed in research)
- Rock Island Trail construction anticipated Spring 2026 (210-foot pedestrian bridge over Sand Creek; 10-12 months) — **HIGH** (coloradosprings.gov/TOPS2026 + KRDO)
- Pikes Peak Greenway Spring 2026 improvements (~0.5 miles new concrete, improved ADA from Corporate Drive/Park and Ride) — **HIGH** (city TOPS page + Gazette)
- Austin Bluffs Spine Trail completion planned 2026 — **HIGH** (city TOPS page)
- Blodgett Open Space ~14 miles new trail — **HIGH** (city TOPS page)
**Do NOT use:**
- Mueller exact distance from downtown (MEDIUM — use "about 35 miles west of COS" only if widely reported; otherwise omit or use "in Divide")
- Specific admission prices for Mueller (not confirmed in research — note day-use fee applies per CPW standard)
